title: Decimals on the number line: thousandths
topic_path: Module 1: Place value and decimal fractions
Topic A: Multiplicative patterns on the place value chart
Decimals on the number line: thousandths

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the value of the point graphed on the number line?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The length between the labeled tick marks is $\blueD{0.01}$, or $\blueD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$. 

Each $\blueD{0.01}$ is divided into $10$ equal pieces that have a length of $\goldD{0.001}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ thousandth}}$.

The point is $\goldD{\text{8 thousandths}}$ after $0.03$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$0.03$ and $\goldD{ \text{8 thousandths}}$ equals ${0.03\goldD8}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths | $\goldD{\text{Thousandths}}$
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: |:-: 
$0$ | $.$ | $0$ |$3$ | $\goldD8$



[[☃ image 1]]
The value of the point graphed on the number line is $0.038$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0.02 to 0.04 with tick marks every one thousandth unit. A point labeled question mark appears eight tick marks after 0.03.

Each $\blueD{0.01}$ is divided into $10$ equal pieces that have a length of $\goldD{0.001}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ thousandth}}$.
${0.08\goldD7}$ is $\goldD{ \text{7 thousandths}}$ to the right of $0.08$ on a number line.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What could be the value of the point graphed on the number line?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. A point between 0.03 and 0.04 is nearer to 0.04.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/493972c79eb4231cb4b0a5d71c2b3c5c437775c4)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The point is between $\greenD{0.03}$ and $\purpleC{0.04}$. 

$\greenD{0.03} < {?} < \purpleC{0.04}$

We can also write the decimals to the thousandths place.

$\greenD{0.030} < {?} < \purpleC{0.040}$

$\greenD{0.030} < {0.072} \,\maroonD{\nless}\, \purpleC{0.040}$

So, $0.072$ is too much.

$\greenD{0.030} < {0.462} \,\maroonD{\nless}\, \purpleC{0.040}$


So, $0.462$ is too much.

$\greenD{0.030} < 0.037 < \purpleC{0.040}$

The value of the graphed point could be $0.037$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What could be the value of the point graphed on the number line?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. A point  is in the middle between 0.09 and 0.10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b53765912fcd0f0a5d4756d33e633d05ce927c45)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The point is between $\greenD{0.09}$ and $\purpleC{0.10}$. 

$\greenD{0.09} < {?} < \purpleC{0.10}$

We can also write the decimals to the thousandths place.

$\greenD{0.090} < {?} < \purpleC{0.100}$

$\greenD{0.090} < {0.950} \,\maroonD{\nless}\, \purpleC{0.100}$

So, $0.950$ is too much.

$\greenD{0.090} \maroonD{\nless} {0.019} < \purpleC{0.100}$


So, $0.019$ is too small.

$\greenD{0.090} < 0.095 < \purpleC{0.100}$

The value of the graphed point could be $0.095$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What could be the value of the point graphed on the number line?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. A point between 0.07 and 0.08 is nearer to 0.07.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e996bd6976772472f5b76d79e29b589545c3d79d)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The point is between $\greenD{0.07}$ and $\purpleC{0.08}$. 

$\greenD{0.07} < {?} < \purpleC{0.08}$

We can also write the decimals to the thousandths place.

$\greenD{0.070} < {?} < \purpleC{0.080}$

$\greenD{0.070} < {0.780} \,\maroonD{\nless}\, \purpleC{0.080}$

So, $0.780$ is too much.

$\greenD{0.070} \maroonD{\nless} {0.027} < \purpleC{0.080}$


So, $0.027$ is too small.

$\greenD{0.070} < 0.072 < \purpleC{0.080}$

The value of the graphed point could be $0.072$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point on the number line represents $0.038$?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. Point A is  between 0.03 and 0.04 and is nearer to 0.04. Point B is  between 0.07 and 0.08 and is nearer to 0.07. Point C is  between 0.08 and 0.09 and is nearer to 0.08.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/26906e1791d2841da719f30366d099c6c490036e)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The number $0.038$ is $38$ thousandths, which is between $\redD{30\text{ thousandths}}$ and $\purpleC{40\text{ thousandths}}$.

$\redD{0.030} < 0.038 < \purpleC{0.040}$
That is the same as saying that $0.038$ is between $\redD{0.03}$ and $\purpleC{0.04}$.

Which point is between $\redD{0.03}$ and $\purpleC{0.04}$?

![The same number line as above.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/26906e1791d2841da719f30366d099c6c490036e)
Point $\blueD{A}$ represents $0.038$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point on the number line represents $0.059$?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. Point A is  between 0.04 and 0.05 and is nearer to 0.05. Point B is  between 0.05 and 0.06 and is nearer to 0.06. Point C is  between 0.09 and 0.10 and is in the middle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8ae68b00e9b5b18019a875575a8145fb54f1391c)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The number $0.059$ is $59$ thousandths, which is between $\redD{50\text{ thousandths}}$ and $\purpleC{60\text{ thousandths}}$.

$\redD{0.050} < 0.059 < \purpleC{0.060}$
That is the same as saying that $0.059$ is between $\redD{0.05}$ and $\purpleC{0.06}$.

Which point is between $\redD{0.05}$ and $\purpleC{0.06}$?

![The same number line as above.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8ae68b00e9b5b18019a875575a8145fb54f1391c)
Point $\maroonD{B}$ represents $0.059$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point on the number line represents $0.084$?**

![A number line from 0 to 0.1, labeled in steps of 0.01. Point A is  between 0.04 and 0.05 and is nearer to 0.04. Point B is  between 0.04 and 0.05 and is nearer to 0.05. Point C is  between 0.08 and 0.09 and is nearer 0.08.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/85fe16edfad466ef9c4ebd1c0598b9238c8602f4)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The number $0.084$ is $84$ thousandths, which is between $\redD{80\text{ thousandths}}$ and $\purpleC{90\text{ thousandths}}$.

$\redD{0.080} < 0.084 < \purpleC{0.090}$
That is the same as saying that $0.084$ is between $\redD{0.08}$ and $\purpleC{0.09}$.

Which point is between $\redD{0.08}$ and $\purpleC{0.09}$?

![The same number line as above.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/85fe16edfad466ef9c4ebd1c0598b9238c8602f4)
Point $\greenD{C}$ represents $0.084$.
